sense.definition: a variation of association football played indoors on a smaller field with walls instead of sidelines

examples.x: Indoor soccer is popular in areas with harsh weather conditions.
He prefers playing indoor soccer during the winter months.
The indoor soccer league has teams from all over the city.
She joined an indoor soccer team to stay active during the off-season.
Indoor soccer requires quick reflexes due to the fast-paced nature of the game.

sense.definition: a fast-paced variant of indoor soccer played with a smaller ball and on a smaller court

examples.x: I play futsal with my friends every weekend at the indoor sports center.
He scored an amazing goal during the futsal match last night.
We’ve been practicing futsal all week to get ready for the tournament.
I love playing futsal because it’s so much fun and helps me stay sharp during the off-season.

sense.definition: a variant of soccer played on sandy beaches with smaller teams and modified rules

examples.x: We formed a team with friends to compete in the local beach soccer league.
She twisted her ankle during a beach soccer game, slipping on the sandy surface.
The rules of beach soccer often vary slightly from traditional soccer.
He represented his country in the beach soccer World Cup.
Beach soccer requires quick reflexes and precise ball control due to the shifting sand.

sense.definition: a modified version of soccer specifically designed for athletes with physical disabilities, played according to adapted rules and regulations

examples.x: His dream of representing his country in Paralympic soccer finally came true.

sense.definition: a variation of soccer specifically designed for visually impaired athletes, played with a ball containing rattles and with players wearing blindfolds

examples.x: Blind soccer is gaining popularity worldwide as a competitive sport for the visually impaired.
Blind soccer requires exceptional auditory awareness and teamwork.
She volunteered as a guide for a blind soccer team, helping players navigate during matches.
The rules of blind soccer prioritize safety and fair play.
Watching blind soccer matches is an inspiring experience, showcasing the determination and talent of visually impaired athletes.

sense.definition: a competitive team sport played by individuals using power wheelchairs, involving maneuvering the ball to score goals

examples.x: She joined a powerchair soccer team to stay active and competitive.
Powerchair soccer requires skillful control of the wheelchair and ball.
He loves the fast-paced action of powerchair soccer.
She improved her coordination through powerchair soccer.
The school introduced powerchair soccer to its adaptive sports program.

sense.definition: a pass sent from the side of the field to the area near the opponent's goal

examples.x: His accurate cross found the striker in front of the goal.
A well-timed cross can catch the defense off guard.
The winger delivered a perfect cross into the penalty area.

sense.definition: a pass made by a player to a teammate positioned behind them, often directed to the goalkeeper

examples.x: The defender made a safe back pass to the goalkeeper.
A risky back pass almost resulted in an own goal.
He used a back pass to maintain possession under pressure.
She executed a precise back pass to her teammate.
The goalkeeper called for a back pass to relieve the pressure.

sense.definition: a short, controlled pass in soccer using the side of the foot to accurately move the ball to a teammate

examples.x: She executed a perfect push pass to her teammate in midfield.
The team's fluid passing game relied heavily on well-timed push passes.
A well-placed push pass can break through even the tightest defenses.
His quick push pass caught the opposing team off guard.
The coach emphasized the importance of precision in push passing during training.

sense.definition: a pass made to a teammate positioned horizontally or laterally from the passer, typically to maintain possession or create space

examples.x: The team prefers sideways passes to control the tempo of the game.
She intercepted the sideways pass and launched a counterattack.
He opted for a sideways pass instead of risking a long ball.
Players are encouraged to look for options for sideways passes to maintain possession.
The coach emphasizes the importance of accurate sideways passes in their playing style.

sense.definition: a move in soccer where a player uses the heel of their foot to pass the ball backward to a teammate

examples.x: He scored with a clever backheel pass.
She executed a perfect backheel pass.
They practiced the backheel pass in training.

sense.definition: a forward pass that goes between or past defenders to reach a teammate in an attacking position

examples.x: He made a perfect through pass.
The through pass split the defense.
He assisted with a brilliant through pass.
The through pass caught the defenders off guard.
Her through pass created a scoring chance.

sense.definition: the act of passing the ball in a particular direction or style to a teammate in soccer

examples.x: The midfielder delivered a long ball over the top.
He lofted a high ball into the path of the striker.
The midfielder pinged a sharp, diagonal ball to switch the point of attack.
They directed a low, driven ball into the penalty area.

sense.definition: a strategic soccer kickoff aimed at quickly recovering the ball before it travels the necessary distance

examples.x: The team attempted an onside kick to catch their opponents off guard.
He executed a perfect onside kick, allowing his team to regain possession.
They practiced the onside kick repeatedly during training.
The onside kick bounced perfectly into the hands of a waiting teammate.
The onside kick attempt failed, resulting in good field position for the opponent.

sense.definition: (soccer) the act of striking the ball with the tip of the foot rather than the instep or side

examples.x: He attempted a toe kick but missed the ball entirely.
They practiced toe kicks to improve their accuracy.
She used a toe kick to nudge the ball forward.
The player resorted to a toe kick when under pressure.
He attempted a long-range toe kick but it went wide.

sense.definition: the act of striking the ball while it is in the air, without letting it touch the ground first

examples.x: He scored with a powerful volley kick from outside the box.
The striker attempted a volley kick but missed the target.
She practiced her volley kick technique during training.
He honed his volley kick skills by practicing against a wall.
The defender cleared the danger with a well-timed volley kick.

sense.definition: (soccer) a free kick awarded to the attacking team when the ball goes out of play over the defending team's goal line

examples.x: The team won a corner kick after the ball was cleared out by the defense.
She took the corner kick and aimed it towards her teammates in the penalty area.
He scored directly from a corner kick, surprising everyone.
The goalkeeper punched away the corner kick to prevent a goal.
They practiced their corner kick strategies during training.

sense.definition: (soccer) a free kick taken from the goal area when the ball crosses the goal line, last touched by an attacker

examples.x: The goalkeeper took a goal kick to clear the ball downfield.
She placed the ball for the goal kick near the edge of the six-yard box.
He launched a powerful goal kick towards the halfway line.
The goalkeeper's goal kick found a teammate near the center circle.
He adjusted his positioning to receive the goalkeeper's goal kick in space.

sense.definition: a direct free kick taken from the penalty spot, awarded after a foul in the penalty area

examples.x: He scored the winning goal with a penalty kick.
The penalty kick hit the crossbar.
She converted the penalty kick with ease.
The goalkeeper saved the penalty kick.
She confidently took the penalty kick.

sense.definition: (soccer) a trick where a player crosses one leg behind the other to kick the ball

examples.x: She scored an amazing goal using a rabona.
The player attempted a rabona but missed.
She practiced the rabona for hours.
The rabona shot hit the post.
She executed a flawless rabona during the match.

sense.definition: a tiebreaker method where players take turns shooting penalty kicks to determine the winner of a match that remains undecided after regular play and extra time

examples.x: The goalkeeper made a crucial save during the penalty shoot-out.
She felt nervous before her turn in the penalty shoot-out.
Their team won the championship after a dramatic penalty shoot-out.
He scored the winning goal in the penalty shoot-out.
The players practiced penalty shoot-outs to improve their skills.

sense.definition: (soccer) a kick that lightly lifts the ball over an opponent, usually the goalkeeper

examples.x: He scored with a clever chip shot.
His chip shot surprised the defense.
She perfected her chip shot during practice.
He attempted a chip shot from the edge of the box.
The chip shot floated into the net.

sense.definition: (soccer) a kick awarded to the attacking team when the ball goes out of play over the defending team's goal line

examples.x: The team won a corner after the defender deflected the ball out of bounds.
She delivered a perfect corner into the penalty area, but the striker missed the header.
The goalkeeper punched away the dangerous corner to clear the ball from the box.
The supporters cheered loudly as their team won a last-minute corner.
The striker positioned himself near the near post to receive the corner.

sense.definition: (soccer) the difference between the total number of goals scored and conceded by a team in a competition

examples.x: Our team won the league due to a superior goal difference.
The tiebreaker was decided by goal difference after both teams drew in points.
They finished second because their goal difference was better than ours.
Even though they lost, their goal difference improved because of their strong defense.
The team needs to win by a large margin to improve their goal difference.

sense.definition: to strike the ball with the head, usually to pass or score in soccer

examples.x: He tried to head the ball into the net.
He rose high to head the ball to his teammate.
The striker managed to head the ball past the goalkeeper.
She practiced how to head the ball during training.
He flicked his neck to head the ball into the goal.

sense.definition: to control or redirect the ball using the chest in soccer

examples.x: He chests the ball to maintain control.
He would chest the ball if given the chance.

sense.definition: to kick the ball a long distance in soccer in an attempt to change field position

examples.x: Facing a challenging defensive situation, the team decided to punt the ball rather than risk turning it over.
The quarterback decided to punt the ball deep into the opponent's territory to reset the field position.
With little time on the clock, the team strategically punted the ball out of bounds.
In an attempt to flip the field, the player skillfully punted the ball over the opposing team's heads.
Unable to advance further, the player chose to punt rather than risk a turnover.
With the game tied in the final minutes, the coach elected to punt and rely on the defense.

sense.definition: (soccer) to skillfully pass or kick the ball through the opponent's legs and regain possession of it on the other side
